data.description:
**Position Summary**
Samsung Research UK (SRUK) is seeking exceptional and highly motivated Interns to join our growing AI team in Staines-upon-Thames. We are at the forefront of developing innovative technologies for future Samsung devices and services, and this role offers a unique opportunity to shape the next generation of mobile experiences. You will be instrumental in translating cutting-edge research into real-world impact, working on challenges that demand both creative problem-solving and robust engineering practices.
  
  
This is a chance to join a dynamic team that is pushing the boundaries of what’s possible with on-device AI, bringing advanced LLMs, RAG systems and Knowledge Graphs onto mobile and server platforms. You will see your work shipped in production and running in the hands of real users. We welcome applications from individuals with strong experience in building and deploying complex machine learning systems, and offer the opportunity to deepen your expertise in a technically challenging and rewarding environment.
  
  
**Role And Responsibilities**
As Intern, you will:
  
  

- Architect and maintain knowledge graphs (KG), leveraging ontologies and graph databases.
- Design and implement RAG and GraphRAG pipelines to enhances reasoning for structured, contextual and dynamic data.
- Evaluate and optimize KG, RAG and GraphRAG systems for accuracy, latency and memory trade-off.
- Finetune and deploy various LLMs and adaptors for on-device applications.
- Build robust data generation and collection pipelines for model training and KG, RAG or GraphRAG construction.
- Contribute code libraries by adding new features and improving its capabilities.
- Evaluate the performance of algorithms and systems through rigorous experimentation and statistical analysis.
- Lead the development of software prototypes and experimental systems, ensuring high code quality and maintainability.
- Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of researchers and engineers to integrate research findings into Samsung products and services.

**Skills And Qualifications**
**Required Skills**

- Currently studying for a PhD in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or a related discipline (or just completing). Masters graduate with relevant knowledge and experience also considered.
- First-author publication in one of the following conferences and journals: NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, EMNLP, CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, AAAI, ACL, IEEE TPAMI, IEEE TNNLS, IJCV, and JMLR.
- Deep technical expertise on Knowledge Graphs: Proficiency in RDF, SPARQL and tools like Neo4j.
- Deep technical expertise on RAG/GraphRAG: Hand-on experience with frameworks like LangChain, LLamaIndex and Vector Databases.
- Deep technical expertise on LLMs: Expertise in fine-tuning, prompt engineering and deploying HuggingFace models and vLLMs.
- Deep technical expertise on Databases: Proficiency in SQL and NoSQL systems, with optimization skills for high-volume data.
- Expert proficiency in Python programming language.
- Proficiency in Pytorch, with experience in NLP tasks.

**Desirable Skills**

- Expert proficiency in Java/Kotlin programming language.
- Previous hands-on experience with deep learning model optimization: quantization, pruning, distillation, or operator-level optimizations.
- Experience with deploying AI models on-device (mobile, embedded, edge accelerators, etc.).

**Contract type:**
5 month internship to start as soon as possible
  
  
**Location:**
Samsung R&D Institute, Staines-upon-Thames, Surrey, UK
  
  
**Hybrid Working:**
3 days onsite (minimum) and 2 days working from home weekly
  
  
Samsung has a strict policy on trade secrets. In applying to Samsung and progressing through the recruitment process, you must not disclose any trade secrets of a previous employer.
  
  

- Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location, at: https://account.samsung.com/membership/policy/privacy. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here: https://europe-samsung.com/ghrp/PrivacyNoticeforEU.html

company_object.name: Samsung Electronics UK
company_object.long_description:
Welcome to Samsung. 

For over 70 years, Samsung has been dedicated to making a better world through diverse businesses that today span advanced technology, semiconductors, skyscraper and plant construction, petrochemicals, fashion, medicine, finance, hotels and more. Our flagship company, Samsung Electronics, leads the global market in high-tech electronics manufacturing and digital media.

Through innovative, reliable products and services; talented people; a responsible approach to business and global citizenship; and collaboration with our partners and customers, Samsung is taking the world in imaginative new directions.

data.description:
**About Samsung Austin Semiconductor**  
Samsung is a world leader in advanced semiconductor technology, founded on the belief that the pursuit of excellence creates a better world. At Samsung Austin Semiconductor, we are Innovating Today to Power the Devices of Tomorrow.

**Come innovate with us!**

**Position Summary**

T1 Manufacturing Quality & Reliability (MQR) Engineer plays a critical role in various qualification processes for new Samsung Taylor FAB and in ensuring that new technologies/products being introduced meet all quality and reliability requirements prior to starting volume mass production. This position bridges internal teams across technology development, process integration, process/EQP engineering, sales, customer, and manufacturing technology during transition from new FAB/technology/product qualification phase to volume production phase in a highly complex semiconductor industry environment to deliver products on time to our customers that exceed their quality requirements. After successful qualification, T1 MQR Engineer will continue to support different quality management activities to secure and maintain expected mass production quality.

**Role and Responsibilities**

**Here’s What You’ll Be Responsible For:**

- Lead various qualification processes to qualify T1 FAB for mass production by conducting rigorous risk analysis and ensuring 100% adherence to global quality standards
- Drive seamless transition from new FAB qualification phase to mass production phase by validating process stability and delivering Fab qualification reports that certify production readiness
- Establish product/tool qualification framework and chair review board meetings to provide technical direction and final decision-making on qualification status
- Communicate with HQ reference FAB to monitor and work to eliminate any known quality risk points by implementing various solutions
- Plan and lead early production risk mitigation activities designed to ensure safe launch of new products
- Contribute to identifying potential quality risk points by using FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) methodology and implementing solutions to reduce risk
- Drive root cause corrective actions (RCCA/8D) for issues identified during FAB qualification phase to secure mass production quality standards

**Skills and Qualifications**

**Here's what you'll need:**

- Bachelor's, Master's, or PhD degree in Electrical Engineering, Physics, Chemical Engineering, Materials Science, Applied Mathematics or related field required
- Strong knowledge of semiconductor processes device physics, and failure analysis techniques
- Familiarity with quality tools and methodologies: SPC, FMEA, DOE, 8D problem-solving, and statistical analysis
- Understanding of industry standards and customer requirements in semiconductors (e.g., automotive, consumer, data center)
- Working knowledge of process integration, yield and/or various fab processes (i.e., thin films deposition, lithography, etch, diffusion etc.)

*The current base salary range for this role is between $76,000 - $145,000. Individual base pay rates will depend on factors including duties, work location, education, skills, qualifications and experience. Total compensation for this position will include a competitive benefits package and may include participation in company incentive compensation programs, which are based on factors to include organizational and individual performance.*

**Total Rewards**  
At Samsung Austin Semiconductor, base pay is just one part of our total compensation package. The base compensation for this role will depend on education, experience, skills, and location.

We offer a comprehensive benefits package, including:

- Medical, dental, and vision insurance
- Life insurance and 401(k) matching with immediate vesting
- Onsite café(s) and workout facilities
- Paid maternity and paternity leave
- Paid time off (PTO) + 2 personal holidays and 10 regular holidays
- Wellness incentives and MORE

Eligible full-time employees (salaried or hourly) may also receive MBO bonuses based on company, division, and individual performance.

All positions at Samsung Austin Semiconductor are full-time on-site.

**U.S. Export Control Compliance**  
This role may require access to information subject to U.S. export control laws. Applicants must be authorized to access such information or eligible for government authorization.

**Trade Secrets Notice**  
By submitting an application, you agree not to disclose to Samsung—or encourage Samsung to use—any confidential or proprietary information (including trade secrets) belonging to a current or former employer or other entity.

- Samsung Electronics America, Inc. and its subsidiaries are committed to employing a diverse workforce, and provide Equal Employment Opportunity for all individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, age, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a protected veteran, genetic information, status as a qualified individual with a disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.

company_object.name: Samsung Electronics
company_object.long_description:
Samsung Electronics is a global leader in technology, opening new possibilities for people everywhere. Through relentless innovation and discovery, we are transforming the worlds of TVs, smartphones, wearable devices, tablets, digital appliances, network systems, medical devices, semiconductors and LED solutions. Samsung is also leading in the Internet of Things space through, among others, our Smart Home and Digital Health initiatives. 

Since being established in 1969, Samsung Electronics has grown into one of the world’s leading technology companies, and become recognized as one of the top 10 global brands. Our network now extends across the world, and Samsung takes great pride in the creativity and diversity of its talented people, who drive our growth.  To discover more, please visit our website at www.samsung.com and our official newsroom at news.samsung.com

data.description:
**Position Summary**

- Enhance company's competitiveness by designing organizations complying with the business strategy and acquiring/nurturing/managing talents.
- Maximize organization's performance and drive employees' motivation by improving/operating HR policy (evaluation/promotion/reward/discipline) complying with the business strategy.
- Create proactive solutions to drive and facilitate change initiatives and continuous improvement projects in support of the customer group and the broader business.

**Role And Responsibilities**
**Position Overview:**
The HR Business Partner (HRBP) Manager is a pivotal role designed for a seasoned HR professional with a proven track record of driving strategic people initiatives. This role support to drive operational effectiveness and shape the future of the business units by leading workforce planning & governance, employee experience enhancement, talent and succession management, and other key people agenda. Reporting to the HR Director, the HRBP will play a critical role in aligning HR strategies with business objectives, fostering a high-performing and engaged workforce, and paving the way for future leadership in HR.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

- Workforce Planning and Governance:

- Develop and execute strategic workforce planning initiatives to align with business goals.
- Ensure effective governance of HR policies and practices across the Commercial Business Units.
- Monitor workforce trends and provide actionable insights to optimize resource allocation.

- Employee Experience Enhancement:

- Design and implement innovative initiatives to improve employee engagement, satisfaction, and retention.
- Foster a positive work culture that promotes collaboration, innovation, and inclusivity.
- Conduct regular employee feedback sessions to identify areas for improvement and drive impactful change.

- Talent and Succession Management:

- Partner with business leaders to identify and develop high-potential employees.
- Design and deliver talent management programs, including leadership development and succession planning.
- Ensure a robust pipeline of future leaders to sustain business growth.

- Strategic Partnership:

- Collaborate with business leaders to understand their needs and provide tailored HR solutions.
- Act as a trusted advisor on all people-related matters, ensuring alignment with organizational goals.

- Organizational Development:

- Lead change management initiatives to support organizational transformation.
- Partner with L&D lead to design and deliver training programs to enhance employee skills and competencies.

- Data Analysis and Reporting:

- Analyze HR metrics to provide insights on workforce performance and employee engagement.
- Prepare regular reports on HR activities and recommend strategies for continuous improvement.

**Qualifications**
**Skills and Qualifications**

- Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
- Master's degree or certifications such as SHRM-CP, SHRM-SCP, or PHR are preferred.
- Minimum of 8 years of experience in HR roles, with at least 5 years in an HRBP position.
- Proven track record of driving strategic HR initiatives in complex, large-scale organizations.
- Strong understanding of workforce planning, talent management, and employee experience enhancement.
- Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
- Proficiency in HRIS systems and data analysis tools.

**Preferred Skills**

- Experience in the commercial or sales sector.
- Ability to manage multiple priorities and work in a fast-paced environment.

**Reporting Structure**

- Reports to: HR Director
- Partners with: Commercial Business Unit Leaders, Employees, and HR Teams
- Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here.

Samsung Electronics is a global leader in technology, opening new possibilities for people everywhere. Through relentless innovation and discovery, we are transforming the worlds of smartphones, TVs, wearable devices, tablets, digital appliances, network systems, medical devices, semiconductors, LED solutions and Internet of Things.

We have grown into one of the world’s leading technology companies, and become recognized as one of the top 10 global brands. Our network now extends across the world, and Samsung takes great pride in the creativity and diversity of its talented people who drive our growth.

In the diverse and vibrant Southeast Asia and Oceania region, our offices are present in Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Myanmar, Cambodia, Laos, Philippines, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand. data.description:
**Position Summary**
Technico-Commercial Sédentaire - Climatisation (H/F)
  
  
Poste en Alternance pour 24 mois
  
  
Rythme d’alternance : 3 semaines en entreprise / 1 semaine en cours de préférence
  
  
Saint-Ouen (93)
  
  
Votre opportunité
  
  
Samsung produit et commercialise des systèmes de climatisation depuis plus de 40 ans, tant pour des applications résidentielles que commerciales.
  
  
Nos 500.000 collaborateurs partagent une véritable culture du leadership, du challenge et de la réussite. Nos équipes sont aussi diversifiées et dynamiques que les produits que nous fabriquons et les marchés sur lesquels nous sommes implantés.
  
  
Grâce à sa présence mondiale, ses téléphones, ses équipements multimédias et de loisirs, Samsung améliore la vie de ses clients sur toute la planète.
  
  
En rejoignant Samsung Electronics, vous participerez au développement de l’activité Climatisation et Chauffage pour la France, permettant à Samsung de devenir un des acteurs majeur en France sur le secteur de la climatisation
  
  
Samsung prévoit un fort développement de son activité Chauffage et Climatisation.
  
  
Une organisation dédiée à la Climatisation a été créé en Europe en 2017, en vue d’adapter aux besoins du marché les processus, les outils et les services.
  
  
Samsung est désormais en mesure d’apporter les produits, les services et supports adaptés aux professionnels de la climatisation, tant d’un point de vue de l’avant-vente, de la logistique et de l’après-vente.
  
  
Pour développer son activité sur le secteur de la climatisation, Samsung souhaite renforcer sa présence commerciale sur la France et pour cela, Samsung recrute un Commercial Sédentaire qui travaille en étroite collaboration avec les commerciaux et assure un service de qualité aux clients.
  
  
**Role And Responsibilities**
**Vos missions**
Rattaché à la Direction Commerciale, vous avez une « fonction support » pour la force de vente.
  
  
Vous apportez une réponse aux questions écrites et/ou téléphoniques des clients professionnels (Installateurs, Négociants, Bureaux d'études..) mais aussi aux équipes en interne (Force de vente, prescription, administration des ventes et marketing produits). Vous aurez un rôle clé de conseils et de services tout en apportant l’offre technique la plus adaptée.
  
  
Dans ce cadre vous :
  
  

- Créez des devis dans le CRM et communiquez la documentation pour les projets étudiés.
- Réalisez des schémas frigorifiques en utilisant les outils internes (DVM PRO)
- Formez ou accompagnez les commerciaux sur l’outil CRM interne (Sales Force)
- Participez aux salons régionaux et nationaux organisées par Samsung
- Réalisez des relances sur des devis sur un périmètre défini par le Manager Commercial
- Echangez avec les « Chargés de produits » et le « Siège Européen » pour trouver les meilleures solutions techniques sur les projets des clients.

**Skills And Qualifications**
**Profil recherché**
Formation BAC +2/+3 : Licence Pro / BTS FED Climatisation et du conditionnement d’air / Systèmes frigorifiques ou BTS/DUT à vocation commerciale (NDRC, MCO, TC ou CI)
  
  

- Vous êtes autonome, dynamique et vous accordez de l’importance au service qui est apporté à vos clients,
- Votre écoute et la gestion des priorités sont vos forces.
- Vous appréciez le travail en équipe, vous êtes enthousiaste, rigoureux et polyvalent
- Vous avez des connaissances techniques dans le domaine climatisation/chauffage
- Vous avez une bonne connaissance des outils informatiques, pack Office
- Votre niveau d’anglais vous permet d’échanger avec notre Siège Européen
- Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location, at: https://account.samsung.com/membership/policy/privacy. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here: https://europe-samsung.com/ghrp/PrivacyNoticeforEU.html

data.job_title: Head of Online Operation & Strategy( Korean Only )
data.location: Ho Chi Minh City, Ho Chi Minh City
data.short_location: Ho Chi Minh City, Ho Chi Minh City
data.long_location: Ho Chi Minh City, Ho Chi Minh City
data.description:
**Position Summary**
As the Head of Operation&Strategy for Online Business, you will be responsible for driving the overall Online strategy, you will lead the BU to ensure excellent sales performance, new business strategy [both cooperative and go-to-market (GTM)], product launch management and SCM, and channel distribution management.
  
  
The role has a broad stakeholder group including HQ, RHQ and the local leadership team. A confident leader is required to navigate the organization to drive significant outcomes. The leader is expected to actively contribute to the broader company strategy and go to market.
  
  

- Eliminate customers' inconveniences in processes before and after purchases including SCM, logistics and CS. Improve back-end processes to encourage customers to revisit.

**Role And Responsibilities**

- Responsible for the delivery of the Online sales target nationally
- Establishing strategic sales & marketing plans to achieve develop and execute marketing programs to achieve stated objectives regarding revenue, profitability, market share and corporate objectives for product category.
- Leading a team of part leaders of Online Operation and bringing Online category to life into the market
- Achieve and exceed Management Plan (MP)
- Expand Samsung market share in the Online sector
- Pro-actively work within the Online sector to enhance Samsung’s reputation within the marketplace
- Align with key partners to educate and enable Samsung’s value proposition so they are proactively positioning Samsung devices to key industry client verticals (via joint-selling, co-marketing and joint-customer events)
- Translating the Go-to-market strategy into actionable, quantitative plans and clear objectives and delivering on sales outcomes
- Coordinate the design and implementation of ranging strategy for the Vietnamese market, and represent Samsung Vietnam in the global development division to ensure product roadmaps match local market requirements.
- Develop with marketing lead optimal integration in pricing and marketing activities and manage marketing funds ensuring optimum utilization and financial control. Manages price roadmap and sales deduction planning for category based on P&L status and target.
- Coordinating the gathering of market intelligence and monitoring competitors' sales strategies and products, campaigns and events to optimize market share
- Build out the sales capability and sales team to deliver profitable sales growth
- Identify and design process improvements, making Samsung simpler and easier to do business with, using innovative and creative thinking to make smarter, simpler and customer centric processes, which are scalable and reduce the need for manual workarounds.
- Other additional duties and responsibilities as directed by management

**Skills And Qualifications**

- Relevant experience in Online sales, go-to-market strategy & sales leadership
- People leadership experience
- High level of business acumen with a strong ability to communicate effectively across all levels of business
- Proven ability to achieve budgeted targets and revenue targets
- Previous experience in building and maintaining customer satisfaction through the development of broad customer relationships, including senior management contact
- Well-developed leadership experience
- Online industry experience would be preferred (especially Shopee/Lazada/Tiktok)
- Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here.

data.description:
**About Samsung Austin Semiconductor**  
Samsung is a world leader in advanced semiconductor technology, founded on the belief that the pursuit of excellence creates a better world. At Samsung Austin Semiconductor, we are Innovating Today to Power the Devices of Tomorrow.

**Come innovate with us!**

**Position Summary**

We are looking for a highly motivated, accountable individual to fill the role of Project PM supporting the Facilities Operations Group. As a Project PM, you’ll be responsible for oversight of the construction including budgeting, schedule, logistics, and ensuring that the construction activities are completed safely and without impact to the manufacturing process.

**Role and Responsibilities**

**Here’s What You’ll Be Responsible For:**

- Construction oversight to ensure work is completed safely and without impact to the process.
- Managing construction processes within operations team.
- Reading and understanding plans and specifications for all phases of construction.
- Excellent written and verbal skills which will allow you to communicate in such a manner that others will clearly understand and comply with requests or information provided.
- Ability to generate schedules using professional scheduling software, experience with Microsoft Projects and Primavera a plus.
- Fluent understanding and capabilities with MS Office, Excel and PowerPoint.
- Ensure job safety by guaranteeing SAS’s safety policies are used in construction, monitoring compliance and developing improvements to existing policy requirements.
- Ensure all projects meet the department’s specifications and requirements for quality.
- Oversight over all projects to be completed on or ahead of schedule.
- Monitor project backlogs and schedules to advise the estimating department to maintain a healthy workload for the company.
- Monitor and analyze job costs to budget.
- Review subcontractor and vendor quotes for accuracy.
- Monitor and review daily reporting of job progress and challenges.

**Skills and Qualifications**

**Here's what you'll need:**

- Qualified candidates will have a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Chemical Engineering.
- 5+ years of experience in facilities, mechanical systems, and chemical systems design and construction highly desired.
- Experience with HVAC/Exhaust systems preferred.
- Experience with Ultra-Pure Waster, Industrial Waste Water a plus.
- Project Management Professional (PMP).
- Professional Engineering (PE) License a flus.
- Experienced in the design, organization, implementation, and management of Construction and Engineering Projects.
- Experienced with the management and execution of federally funded contracts within a commercial organization.
- Experienced in the delegation, tasking, tracking, and reporting of tasks/projects.
- Experienced in the nuances of construction efforts including insurance, bonding, licensing, estimating, and supervising.
- Experience in Civil, Structural & Architectural construction a plus.

*The current base salary range for this role is between $76,000 - $145,000. Individual base pay rates will depend on factors including duties, work location, education, skills, qualifications and experience. Total compensation for this position will include a competitive benefits package and may include participation in company incentive compensation programs, which are based on factors to include organizational and individual performance.*

**Total Rewards**  
At Samsung Austin Semiconductor, base pay is just one part of our total compensation package. The base compensation for this role will depend on education, experience, skills, and location.

We offer a comprehensive benefits package, including:

- Medical, dental, and vision insurance
- Life insurance and 401(k) matching with immediate vesting
- Onsite café(s) and workout facilities
- Paid maternity and paternity leave
- Paid time off (PTO) + 2 personal holidays and 10 regular holidays
- Wellness incentives and MORE

Eligible full-time employees (salaried or hourly) may also receive MBO bonuses based on company, division, and individual performance.

All positions at Samsung Austin Semiconductor are full-time on-site.

**U.S. Export Control Compliance**  
This role may require access to information subject to U.S. export control laws. Applicants must be authorized to access such information or eligible for government authorization.

**Trade Secrets Notice**  
By submitting an application, you agree not to disclose to Samsung—or encourage Samsung to use—any confidential or proprietary information (including trade secrets) belonging to a current or former employer or other entity.

- Samsung Electronics America, Inc. and its subsidiaries are committed to employing a diverse workforce, and provide Equal Employment Opportunity for all individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, age, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a protected veteran, genetic information, status as a qualified individual with a disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.

data.description:
**About Samsung Austin Semiconductor**  
Samsung is a world leader in advanced semiconductor technology, founded on the belief that the pursuit of excellence creates a better world. At Samsung Austin Semiconductor, we are Innovating Today to Power the Devices of Tomorrow.

**Come innovate with us!**

**Position Summary**

The IRP Technician is responsible for monitoring, maintaining, and responding to critical FAB infrastructure and facility systems during shift operations. This role serves as the primary on-site technical resource during business hours and support off-hours and weekends when needed, ensuring rapid response to equipment failures, utility disruptions, and facility alarms to minimize downtime and maintain safe, continuous operations.  
  
The technician independently respond, troubleshoots and repairs facility infrastructure and critical FAB equipment issues. This position requires strong technical expertise, sound judgment under pressure, and the ability to manage multiple concurrent issues with minimal supervision.  
  
The IRP Technician plays a critical role in safeguarding production continuity, ensuring compliance with safety standards, and protecting both personnel and high-value manufacturing assets in a 24/7 FAB environment.

**Role and Responsibilities**

Here’s What You’ll Be Responsible For:

- Monitor FAB infrastructure and facility systems
- Respond immediately to alarms, system faults, and equipment failures to minimize downtime
- Respond, troubleshoot and repair issues independently
- Execute preventive and corrective maintenance on critical facility systems
- Coordinate and communicate with operations, engineering, and safety teams during incidents
- Perform initial containment and escalation for high-risk or complex failures
- Ensure compliance with safety standards, including LOTO, PPE, and emergency procedures
- Document incidents, repairs, and shift activities in maintenance and tracking systems
- Support continuous improvement efforts to enhance system reliability and response efficiency

**Skills and Qualifications**

Here's what you'll need:

- Experience supporting semiconductor FAB infrastructure or similar cleanroom environments
- Plumbing licensed & or Journeyman pipefitter
- Experience with preventive and predictive maintenance programs
- Strong communication skills for cross-team coordination and incident escalation
- Ability to work independently with minimal supervision in high-pressure situations

The current base salary range for this role is between $20.50 - $46.00/hour. Individual base pay rates will depend on factors including duties, work location, education, skills, qualifications and experience. Total compensation for this position will include a competitive benefits package and may include participation in company incentive compensation programs, which are based on factors to include organizational and individual performance.

**Total Rewards**  
At Samsung Austin Semiconductor, base pay is just one part of our total compensation package. The base compensation for this role will depend on education, experience, skills, and location.

We offer a comprehensive benefits package, including:

- Medical, dental, and vision insurance
- Life insurance and 401(k) matching with immediate vesting
- Onsite café(s) and workout facilities
- Paid maternity and paternity leave
- Paid time off (PTO) + 2 personal holidays and 10 regular holidays
- Wellness incentives and MORE

Eligible full-time employees (salaried or hourly) may also receive MBO bonuses based on company, division, and individual performance.

All positions at Samsung Austin Semiconductor are full-time on-site.

**Trade Secrets Notice**  
By submitting an application, you agree not to disclose to Samsung—or encourage Samsung to use—any confidential or proprietary information (including trade secrets) belonging to a current or former employer or other entity.

- Samsung Electronics America, Inc. and its subsidiaries are committed to employing a diverse workforce, and provide Equal Employment Opportunity for all individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, age, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a protected veteran, genetic information, status as a qualified individual with a disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.

data.description:
**Position Summary**

1. Enhance company's competitiveness by designing organizations complying with the business strategy and acquiring/nurturing/managing talents.  
2. Maximize organization's performance and drive employees' motivation by improving/operating HR policy (evaluation/promotion/reward/discipline) complying with the business strategy.  
3. Create proactive solutions to drive and facilitate change initiatives and continuous improvement projects in support of the customer group and the broader business.

**Role and Responsibilities**

**Position Overview:** The HR Business Partner (HRBP) Manager is a pivotal role designed for a seasoned HR professional with a proven track record of driving strategic people initiatives. This role support to drive operational effectiveness and shape the future of the business units by leading workforce planning & governance, employee experience enhancement, talent and succession management, and other key people agenda. Reporting to the HR Director, the HRBP will play a critical role in aligning HR strategies with business objectives, fostering a high-performing and engaged workforce, and paving the way for future leadership in HR.

**Key Responsibilities:**

- **Workforce Planning and Governance:**
  - Develop and execute strategic workforce planning initiatives to align with business goals.
  - Ensure effective governance of HR policies and practices across the Commercial Business Units.
  - Monitor workforce trends and provide actionable insights to optimize resource allocation.
- **Employee Experience Enhancement:**
  - Design and implement innovative initiatives to improve employee engagement, satisfaction, and retention.
  - Foster a positive work culture that promotes collaboration, innovation, and inclusivity.
  - Conduct regular employee feedback sessions to identify areas for improvement and drive impactful change.
- **Talent and Succession Management:**
  - Partner with business leaders to identify and develop high-potential employees.
  - Design and deliver talent management programs, including leadership development and succession planning.
  - Ensure a robust pipeline of future leaders to sustain business growth.
- **Strategic Partnership:**
  - Collaborate with business leaders to understand their needs and provide tailored HR solutions.
  - Act as a trusted advisor on all people-related matters, ensuring alignment with organizational goals.
- **Organizational Development:**
  - Lead change management initiatives to support organizational transformation.
  - Partner with L&D lead to design and deliver training programs to enhance employee skills and competencies.
- **Data Analysis and Reporting:**
  - Analyze HR metrics to provide insights on workforce performance and employee engagement.
  - Prepare regular reports on HR activities and recommend strategies for continuous improvement.

**Skills and Qualifications**

**Qualifications:**

- Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
- Master's degree or certifications such as SHRM-CP, SHRM-SCP, or PHR are preferred.
- Minimum of 8 years of experience in HR roles, with at least 5 years in an HRBP position.
- Proven track record of driving strategic HR initiatives in complex, large-scale organizations.
- Strong understanding of workforce planning, talent management, and employee experience enhancement.
- Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
- Proficiency in HRIS systems and data analysis tools.

**Preferred Skills:**

- Experience in the commercial or sales sector.
- Ability to manage multiple priorities and work in a fast-paced environment.

**Reporting Structure:**

- Reports to: HR Director
- Partners with: Commercial Business Unit Leaders, Employees, and HR Teams

#Li-Midsenior #Li-Savina

data.description:
**Position Summary**

Why join our team?  
  
This role sits at the centre of the Mobile Experience (MX) Division within Samsung Electronics UK & Ireland. As Strategy, Operations & Supply Chain Director, you will lead the teams responsible for Strategy & Business Management, Consumer & Market Insights and Supply Chain, providing the strategic direction, operational governance and execution capability required to deliver Samsung's commercial ambitions. Acting as the control tower for the MX business, you will connect long-term strategy with day-to-day execution, ensuring leadership decisions are informed by robust insight, business performance is proactively managed and customers receive the right products at the right time.  
  
This is a critical leadership role, shaping how the organisation plans, prioritises and performs. You will work directly with the UK Leadership Team and regional headquarters to define strategic priorities, drive operational excellence and build the capabilities required to deliver sustainable growth across Smartphones, Tablets, Wearables, Audio and Ecosystem products. As a senior leader, you will influence major business decisions, sponsor strategic transformation initiatives and ensure the organisation maintains a clear focus on commercial performance, customer experience and operational execution.

**Role and Responsibilities**

**Your key responsibilities**

Strategy & Business Management

- Define and maintain the mid and long-term strategic direction of the UK & Ireland MX business, ensuring alignment with European and Global priorities.
- Lead annual business planning, long-range planning and strategic review processes, translating business objectives into actionable plans and measurable outcomes.
- Act as a trusted advisor to the UK Leadership Team, providing insight, challenge and recommendations to support key business decisions.
- Own the governance framework for business performance, ensuring clear visibility of revenue, profitability, market share, operational KPIs and strategic objectives.
- Lead executive performance reviews, business reporting and operational governance processes to identify risks, opportunities and corrective actions.
- Manage and prioritise strategic initiatives that accelerate growth, improve competitiveness and strengthen Samsung's market position.
- Lead relationships with external research agencies, consultancies and insight partners, ensuring the business maintains a market-leading understanding of customer behaviour and market dynamics.
- Sponsor and lead cross-functional taskforces established to address critical business challenges and opportunities.
- Develop future organisational capabilities by embedding best practice across strategy development, performance management and business governance.

Operations

- Provide strategic leadership and vision across the operational functions of the MX business, ensuring alignment between strategic priorities and execution.
- Drive the operating rhythm of the UK MX business, ensuring effective alignment across commercial, operational and strategic planning cycles.
- Champion data-driven decision making through the development of reporting tools, performance dashboards and business analytics capabilities.
- Lead cross-functional transformation programmes focused on simplification, automation and operational excellence.
- Partner closely with Sales, Marketing, Product Management, Finance and European Headquarters to ensure cohesive execution of strategic priorities.
- Ensure the organisation maintains strong planning discipline, governance standards and accountability across all business functions.
- Drive process improvements that increase organisational effectiveness, improve decision-making quality and reduce complexity.
- Establish scalable business processes, governance frameworks and reporting structures that support future growth.

Supply Chain & Planning

- Lead UK & Ireland logistics and distribution functions, ensuring effective demand planning, supply planning, inventory management and fulfilment performance across all channels.
- Drive forecasting excellence through robust planning processes, scenario modelling and cross-functional collaboration with Product Management, Sales and Headquarters.
- Balance customer requirements, commercial priorities and operational constraints to optimise business outcomes while maintaining healthy inventory levels.
- Drive continuous improvement in forecast accuracy, inventory health, service levels and customer fulfilment performance.
- Ensure strong collaboration between commercial teams and supply chain functions to maximise revenue opportunities and minimise operational risk.
- Develop planning capabilities, tools and governance that improve visibility, responsiveness and execution quality across the business.

**Your key people management responsibilities**

- Define a clear vision, key focus areas and operating principles for the Strategy, Operations & Supply Chain organisation, communicating regularly to ensure alignment and engagement.
- Lead and develop senior managers across Strategy & Business Management, Consumer & Market Insights and Supply Chain functions, providing coaching, support and accountability.
- Foster a high-performance culture focused on ownership, collaboration, continuous improvement and commercial impact.
- Ensure clarity, governance and consistency across all people management processes, including objective setting, performance reviews, development planning and talent management.
- Build organisational capability through succession planning, mentoring and leadership development programmes.
- Promote cross-functional collaboration and knowledge sharing across teams to improve organisational effectiveness and decision-making quality.
- Ensure compliance with SEUK policies, processes and governance standards while maintaining a strong focus on employee engagement and wellbeing.
- Create an environment where individuals are empowered to challenge, innovate and continuously improve how the business operates.

**What we need for this role**

To be successful, you will possess the following skills and attributes:

- Extensive senior leadership experience within strategy, operations, supply chain, commercial management or business transformation roles.
- Demonstrated experience leading large, cross-functional teams within a complex consumer electronics, technology or FMCG organisation.
- Deep understanding of strategic planning, business management, commercial performance and operational governance.
- Proven experience influencing executive stakeholders and driving alignment across diverse functions and leadership teams.
- Strong understanding of demand planning, forecasting, inventory management and end-to-end supply chain operations.
- Exceptional analytical capability with the ability to translate complex data into actionable business recommendations.
- Strong commercial and financial acumen, with experience balancing short-term performance requirements against long-term strategic objectives.
- Demonstrated success leading organisational transformation, process improvement and capability-building initiatives.
- Outstanding communication and stakeholder management skills, with experience working closely with regional and global headquarters teams.
- Proven track record of developing high-performing teams and building future leadership capability.

**What does success look like?**

- Within 6 months, the MX division operates with clear strategic priorities, effective governance and strong alignment across commercial, operational and planning functions.
- Leadership decisions are supported by high-quality insight, performance visibility and robust strategic recommendations.
- Business performance management is proactive, with clear ownership of risks, opportunities and delivery against key objectives.
- Forecast accuracy, inventory health and customer fulfilment performance continue to improve through stronger planning processes and cross-functional collaboration.
- Strategic initiatives deliver measurable business impact and contribute to sustainable revenue, profit and market share growth.
- Consumer and market insights become increasingly embedded within business decision-making and strategic planning processes.
- The Strategy, Operations & Supply Chain division is recognised as a trusted partner to the Leadership Team and a key driver of business performance.
- Cross-functional collaboration improves, organisational complexity is reduced and operational processes become more efficient and scalable.
- Within 12 months, the function is recognised as the strategic and operational control tower for the UK & Ireland MX business, providing the clarity, capability and leadership required to deliver Samsung's long-term ambitions.

**Skills and Qualifications**

**Benefits of working at Samsung include**

- Bonus scheme linked to individual, team and company performance
- Car allowance
- Private medical – family cover
- Income protection
- Enhanced pension contribution
- Three volunteering days each year
- Flexible benefit portal and contribution of £55 per month - including travel insurance, gym membership, season ticket loan, Tastecard, dental insurance and more
- Holiday - 25 days plus bank holidays and an additional day off for your birthday
- Access to discounts on a wide range of Samsung products
- Access to a discount shopping portal

**A note on equal opportunities**

We are an equal-opportunity employer and value diversity at our Company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

data.description:
**Position Summary**
Headquartered in Englewood Cliffs, N.J., Samsung Electronics America, Inc. (SEA), the U.S. Sales and Marketing subsidiary, is a leader in mobile technologies, consumer electronics, home appliances, enterprise solutions and networks systems. For more than four decades, Samsung has driven innovation, economic growth and workforce opportunity across the United States—investing over $100 billion and employing more than 20,000 people nationwide. By integrating our large portfolio of products, services and AI technology, we’re creating smarter, sustainable and more connected experiences that empower people to live better. SEA is a wholly owned subsidiary of Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.
  
  
To learn more, visit Samsung.com. For the latest news, visit news.samsung.com/us.
  
  
**Role And Responsibilities**
The Samsung North America Shared Service Center Payroll Team is a dynamic, high-energy team providing top-tier payroll management. If you are a customer service-oriented team player at heart, with keen attention to detail and a passion for problem solving, there is no better place for you than Samsung!
  
  
As a Payroll Tax & Audit Manager, one of your key objectives in supporting our high-volume processing is to improve accuracy and increase efficiency. In this role, we challenge you to bring your best on a daily basis and welcome your ideas for how to do things differently and better. You will use your well-rounded problem-solving skills to creatively coordinate payroll tax and audit support activities and collaborate with Samsung teams to foster an environment in which continuous improvement is the standard.
  
  
In pursuit of success, the NA SSC Payroll Team works cross-functionally with internal partners including Human Resources, HRIS and Benefits to ensure accurate and timely processing of payroll and related transactions.
  
  
At Samsung, you have the freedom to provide the expert support that makes our solutions stand out in an increasingly competitive global marketplace.
  
  

- Fulfill day-to-day payroll tax and audit management research in support of various payroll service team projects and continuous improvement initiatives
- Develop and maintain various excel and Access “databases” to support audit administration and payroll processes.
- Ensure data integrity and compliance with regulatory standards for payroll tax management
- Effectively manage relationships within defined SLAs while providing on-going research, resolution, product support and a superior customer experience to workers, HR, accounting, retirement program and benefits administrators, as well as payroll and timekeeping vendors
- Evaluate processes and procedural documents for increased efficiencies and opportunities for continuous improvement
- Support Payroll Team in delivering high-quality results to payroll customers
- Support reoccurring and ad hoc reporting including production of basic to intermediate reporting to various internal and external stakeholders
- Support continuous, periodic and cumulative audit processes for monthly, quarterly and annual activities
- Perform real-time data stream comparative audits and deliver fall out reports to payroll team, subsidiary stakeholders and/or third-party administrators for expedited resolution
- Expertly interpret, create and present a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, and diagram or schedule form to technical and non-technical stakeholders
- Other duties as assigned

**Skills And Qualifications**

- 6+ years of US & CAN payroll tax management experience preferably in a 3rd party payroll or shared service environment
- Ability to demonstrate strong customer relationship skills
- Experience with top tier payroll, HRIS and accounting systems (UltiPro, Workday, SAP or similar)
- Ability to troubleshoot and resolve requests using pre-defined and creative solutions
- Expertly interpret, create and present a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, and diagram or schedule form
- Produce measurable results in a collaborative team environment as well as independently to manage time and priorities while achieving defined personal and team goals
- Payroll audit, control, quality assurance and/or tax management experience
- Payroll accounting experience including GL posting, accruals and reconciliation of payroll expenses
- Demonstrate intermediate knowledge of Microsoft Office Products, specifically Excel, Word, PowerPoint and Access

**Key Results Areas**

- Identify and resolve payroll tax exposure areas including registration of accounts, tax audit coordination and tax amendment tracking
- Develop and maintain payroll audit related databases
- Support payroll team Service Request Ticket resolution SLA at or above current SSC requirements
- Ensure key audit and quality control activities are completed according to established schedule and guidelines
- Support team projects and continuous improvement initiative

**Preferred Qualifications:**

- Bachelor's degree preferred
- Basic familiarity with SQL query writing preferred

Life @ Samsung - https://www.samsung.com/us/careers/life-at-samsung/
  
  
Regular full-time employees (salaried or hourly) have access to benefits including: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, 401(k), Employee Purchase Program, Tuition Assistance (after 6 months), Paid Time Off, Student Loan Program (after 6 months), Wellness Incentives, and many more. In addition, regular full-time employees (salaried or hourly) are eligible for MBO bonus compensation, based on company, division, and individual performance.
  
  

- Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here.

At Samsung, we believe that innovation and growth are driven by an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce. We aim to create a global team where everyone belongs and has equal opportunities, inspiring our talent to be their true selves. Together, we are building a better tomorrow for our customers, partners, and communities.
  
  

- Samsung Electronics America, Inc. and its subsidiaries are committed to employing a diverse workforce, and provide Equal Employment Opportunity for all individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, age, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a protected veteran, genetic information, status as a qualified individual with a disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.

**Reasonable Accommodations for Qualified Individuals with Disabilities During the Application Process**
Samsung Electronics America is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application process. If you have a disability and require a reasonable accommodation in order to participate in the application process, please contact our Reasonable Accommodation Team (855-557-3247) or SEA\_Accommodations\_Ext@sea.samsung.com for assistance. This number is for accommodation requests only and is not intended for general employment inquiries.

company_object.name: Samsung Electronics America
company_object.long_description:
Headquartered in Ridgefield Park, N.J., Samsung Electronics America, Inc. (SEA) pushes beyond the limits of today’s technology to provide groundbreaking connected experiences across its large portfolio of products and services, including mobile devices, home appliances, home entertainment, 5G networks, and digital displays.

We’re committed to keeping co-prosperity at our core through creating human-centric innovation, empowering the next generation and serving the communities where we live and work. As an eleven-time winner of EPA’s ENERGY STAR® Partner of the Year Award for Sustained Excellence, SEA is dedicated to positively impacting the environment through its eco-conscious products, practices and operations.

data.description:
**Job Responsibilities :**  
**Responsibilities:**  

1. AX Strategic Planning

- Develop  **AI-based productivity and innovation strategies**  aligned with business priorities
- Derive insights through  **technology trends, market trends, and competitor analysis**
- Establish  **mid- to long-term AI-driven productivity improvement roadmaps**  and execution strategies
- Identify and translate emerging AI and digital technologies into  **practical business and service applications**
- Support leadership decision-making through structured insights and strategic recommendations

1. AX Performance Management

- Review and manage the  **overall status of AX projects** , including consolidated project reviews
- Manage  **project progress, milestones, risks, and dependencies**  across AX initiatives
- Track, monitor, and verify performance against  **monthly, quarterly, and annual targets**
- Verify task inputs and execution status by division within the  **AX performance management system**
- Design and assess  **ROI and value realization**  using quantitative metrics (e.g. cost, manpower, operational efficiency)
- Prepare structured  **performance reports and dashboards**  for stakeholders and leadership

1. AX Culture & Capability Building

- Develop  **AI literacy enhancement strategies and roadmaps**  for employees
- Plan and design  **customized AI learning programs and curricula**  based on different proficiency levels
- Conduct **planning and quality control (QC)** of AI education programs and learning materials
- Plan, write, and distribute  **internal and external communications** , including AX service introductions and promotions
- Drive AI adoption through learning, communication, and change enablement initiatives

**Requirements:**  

1. Experience & Knowledge

- Relevant experience in developing  **AI-based productivity or innovation strategies**
- Experience analyzing  **new technology trends**  and applying them to business or service improvement
- Proven project experience in Machine Learning (ML) or Large Language Model (LLM) related initiatives
- Familiarity with AI technologies, data platforms, and digital transformation frameworks.

1. Skills & Competencies

- Strong capability in  **data-driven problem analysis** , deriving improvement measures, and implementing solutions
- Ability to design and analyze  **ROI using quantitative and qualitative performance metrics**
- Strong communication and collaboration skills with  **domestic and international stakeholders**
- Competence in preparing  **management and performance reports**  with clear insights
- Experience in planning and delivering  **internal training programs or workshops**
- Structured, execution-oriented mindset with strong coordination skills

1. Preferred / Nice to Have

- Experience in company-wide transformation, productivity, or digital initiatives (i.e. from ground-up or further system & strategy development)
- Exposure to learning program design, change management, or enterprise enablement
- Ability to design or oversee  **visual and educational content**  for internal communications

**More Details**  

Employment TypeFull Time

Required Level of StudyBachelor degree

Closing Date31 Jul 2026

Areas of WorkEngineering

**Company Profile**  

Samsung

Inspire the World, Create the Future

Engineering Singapore
View Employer Profile

company_object.name: Samsung Asia Pte Ltd
company_object.long_description:
Samsung Electronics is a global leader in technology, opening new possibilities for people everywhere. Through relentless innovation and discovery, we are transforming the worlds of smartphones, TVs, wearable devices, tablets, digital appliances, network systems, medical devices, semiconductors, LED solutions and Internet of Things.
We have grown into one of the world’s leading technology companies, and become recognized as one of the top 10 global brands. Our network now extends across the world, and Samsung takes great pride in the creativity and diversity of its talented people who drive our growth.
In the diverse and vibrant Southeast Asia and Oceania region, our offices are present in Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Myanmar, Cambodia, Laos, Philippines, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand. We support the digital ambitions of the region by working closely with local partners as part of our commitment to give back to the communities we operate in.
Visit www.samsung.com to learn more about our business, and www.samsung.com/careers to learn more about career opportunities. Join us as we Reimagine Your Future.
About Samsung Electronics Singapore
Back in 1990, Samsung Electronics established our subsidiary in Singapore. From consumer electronics to digital appliances and IT and mobile, we have become a major consumer brand in the region, bringing consumer satisfaction through our quality products. We aim to inspire the world with our innovations that enrich people’s lives and the community we are present in.
As part of 8 subsidiaries within Southeast Asia and Oceania, the Singapore office also houses the Regional Headquarters.
